title: Bargain with a smile
pedagogy: Vocabulary: ลด (low ล + dead-short inherent o + ด → high). Capstone recombination: ลด + หน่อย (unit 5) + ได้ไหม (units 2+6) + particle = the polite counter-offer. Register: markets bargain, malls don't; softeners over stone-facing.

exercises.title: Bargaining is a smile sport
body: Markets expect a counter-offer; malls and 7-Elevens don't. Read the room: price tags mean fixed, a vendor's guess means game on.
The polite counter is a question, not a demand: ลดหน่อยได้ไหม — “could you come down a little?”.
Every word in it is a rerun: ลด lower, หน่อย a-little, ได้ can, ไหม question. New sentence, zero new grammar.

exercises.meaning: Could you lower it a bit? (male speaker)
exercises.gloss: lower + softener + can + question particle + male polite particle
exercises.explanation: Five chips, four old friends. ได้ไหม “can you?” is the polite hinge — the vendor answers ได้ or ไม่ได้, and the dance begins.

exercises.context: A market vendor quotes 500 baht for a shirt you like. You're a woman. Open the negotiation politely.
exercises.question: What do you say?

choices.thai: ลดหน่อยได้ไหมคะ

exercises.explanation: The question form keeps everyone smiling. Bare แพง is theater (usable, but bold), and ไม่เอา ends the conversation instead of starting it.
